description_long: This is a modern military jet aircraft with a sleek, aerodynamic fuselage and twin vertical stabilizers at the rear. The wings are sharply swept back and mounted low on the fuselage, each equipped with underwing pylons carrying external fuel tanks or ordnance. The aircraft features a bubble canopy cockpit positioned forward on the fuselage, providing excellent visibility for the pilot. The twin-engine nacelles are integrated into the rear fuselage, with large exhaust nozzles visible at the back. The surface is finished in a matte gray camouflage scheme with some insignia and markings on the wings and tail. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it is likely retractable and stowed within the fuselage and wings.
description: A sleek, twin-engine military jet with swept-back low wings, twin vertical stabilizers, and a forward bubble canopy cockpit in matte gray camouflage.
description_view_0: Rear view showing twin engine exhausts, twin vertical stabilizers, and the full span of the swept-back wings.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the low-mounted swept wings, external stores, and forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the pointed nose, bubble canopy, and symmetrical wing and stabilizer arrangement.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to view 1, showing the aircraft's markings, wing configuration, and engine placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, sharply swept with underwing pylons and no visible winglets; moderate anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the rear fuselage with visible exhaust nozzles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with twin vertical stabilizers and horizontal stabilizers mounted low on the tail.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, inferred from typical configuration and lack of visible gear).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed bubble canopy cockpit, likely single or tandem seat with wide visibility.
